programmes.show_description: Iron Guts Kelly: Famed General 'Iron Guts' Kelly arrives for an inspection and dies in action, but not the kind of death that would look good on a war record.
O.R.: The wounded arrive by helicopter, ambulance and jeep, leaving the 4077th overwhelmed by the casualties. Everyone is called into action while Hawkeye, Trapper and Henry work quickly.
Springtime: Several wild events happen during a spring day in Korea. Klinger receives a letter from his girl Laverne who agrees to marry him. Next, Radar falls for a nurse.
Check-up: All personnel are ordered to have a medical check-up. Trapper refuses an examination and finally confesses he's got an ulcer which could provide grounds for a medical discharge.
Leaving: Matthew is set to leave home for college, and Henry is far from sad, as he's been looking forward to this day for some time.
A New Life: Henry Willows has been divorced for several years and is happy and content living alone. But his home life is quickly turned upside down when his son Matthew asks to stay.
Rumpole on Trial: Rumpole is in an irritable mood after visiting the dentist. But will his temper get the better of him in court during a manslaughter case? Peter Sallis stars.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Jonathan helps Kim, a nurse at a Marine Corps post, who still suffers emotional wounds from Vietnam. Meanwhile, a teenager waiting to enter pilot training finds out he's adopted.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Hello And Farewell: Part 2: Kim agrees to get help at the veterans' counselling centre group, and Jonathan reassures Major Hastings about his son.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
A Dolphin Song For Lee: Part 1: A teenage cancer patient is dying, and has one more chance for a lifesaving operation. She has one dream that she would like to see fulfilled.
A Dolphin Song For Lee: Part 2: Lee undergoes an operation which doctors hope will cure her cancer. Mark helps her lobby in the state senate for broader environmental protection.
Ma's Holiday: On a second honeymoon trip with Charles, Caroline can't help worrying about what's happening back home. Mr Edwards finds babysitting three active little girls is not simple.
School Mom: Caroline teaches a shy, older boy to read while she substitutes for an injured Miss Beadle. But it is ruined when mean-spirited Mrs Oleson humiliates him in front of the class.
The Raccoon: When Laura's doll breaks, Mary gives her a baby raccoon. When it bites the Ingalls' dog, Charles watches for signs of rabies, and a tearful Mary reveals Jasper also bit Laura.
The Night Walker: A mysterious presence moving through the night strikes fear in the hearts of the Walton's Mountain residents.
The Cloudburst: In a desperate attempt to save his newspaper from bankruptcy, John-Boy sells his meadow to a mining company. But will the company's plans for the land devastate the area?
The Odd Couple: There is much curiosity over the new love in Donal's life, but Suzie proves to be a wild and dangerous female.
The Last Hurrah: Part 2: Tom and Deborah are greeted by the political fallout of Jean and Eddie impersonating them. Meanwhile, Rev. Brewer finalises Archie's funeral arrangements.
Mercury: A woman becomes convinced she has contracted syphilis from her husband, who indignantly claims he's never had an affair.
Rumpole and the Married Lady: Rumpole accepts a divorce case involving his friend and colleague George Frobrisher. Meanwhile, his Chambers is embroiled in a petty theft case.
Love and Marriage: Frank blames a young Korean assistant, Mr Kwan, for his mistakes in the OR. Hawkeye and Trapper befriend Kwan and try to reunite him with his pregnant wife.
Big Mac: The camp prepares for a visit from General Douglas MacArthur, but not all personnel are excited; Klinger, in his drag, is told to keep out of sight.
Payday: Hawkeye has been given the job of acting paymaster. When the job is done, Hawkeye slips Radar a left over 10 dollars which prompts the wrath of a zealous payroll clerk.
White Gold: Thieves attempt to steal penicillin from the 4077th but Klinger catches one of them. Hawkeye and Trapper are suspicious when Colonel Flagg of the CIA arrives to investigate.
Abyssinia, Henry: Radar brings a message to Colonel Henry Blake; he's been discharged from the army. Everyone at the 4077th prepares to say goodbye to him as he heads for home.
Welcome to Korea - Part 1: Hawkeye returns from R&R to find Trapper has been discharged. He drives Radar at breakneck speed to the airport, but Trapper's plane has gone.
Welcome to Korea - Part 2: Hawkeye misses his chance to say goodbye to Trapper but picks up a replacement - good-natured BJ. On the road they see a woman injured in a minefield.
Sleeper: The team find themselves racing against time on a mission to save London when a new form of gas falls into the wrong hands.
False Witness: Steed investigates a blackmailer whose victims have either been killed or lost the ability to tell the truth. Can Steed trust anyone involved in the case, including Tara?
Love and Marriage: Frank blames a young Korean assistant, Mr Kwan, for his mistakes in the OR. Hawkeye and Trapper befriend Kwan and try to reunite him with his pregnant wife.
Big Mac: The camp prepares for a visit from General Douglas MacArthur, but not all personnel are excited; Klinger, in his drag, is told to keep out of sight.
Payday: Hawkeye has been given the job of acting paymaster. When the job is done, Hawkeye slips Radar a left over 10 dollars which prompts the wrath of a zealous payroll clerk.
Bad Apples: Insisting that his son finishes his education, Henry approaches his old headmaster about Matthew enrolling as a pupil at his school. But Matthew has different ideas.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Silent Bell: A minister faces a crisis of conscience and an exodus of ethnically mixed teachers and students when his church decrees that religion be taught in his preschool.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Reunion: It's Mark's high school reunion. His ageing comrades from High School must come to terms with the fact that they're not the popular young people that they used to be.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Heaven Nose, Mister Smith: Bob Hope guest stars as an angel dispatcher who assigns Jonathan to stop an angel intent on breaking up his son's marriage.
The Whole Nine Yards: A chauvinistic coach refuses to have girls on his high school football team.
The Voice of Tinker Jones: Reverend Alden's request for a new church bell starts a feud between the Kennedy's and Oleson's.
The Award: Mary is banned from taking a scholarship exam for accidentally setting fire to the barn.
The Lord is My Shepherd: Part 1: Jealous of Charles' attention for his new son, Laura longs for her Pa's affections and refuses to pray for her baby brother to get well. Part 1 of 2.
The Great Motorcycle Race: Jim-Bob takes his first steps into the world when he enters a race, riding Ike's motorcycle.
The Pony Cart: The oldest living Walton, Aunt Martha Corrine, travels to Walton's Mountain for an extended visit.
Turf: The villagers are all at the beach for the annual horse race, usually won by Doctor Ryan. However, this year there is stiff competition from a mystery horse owned by Eamon.
A Very Ormston Christmas: It's Christmas time in Ormston, and the locals prepare for the festive period. Arthur is in a playful mood, and Deborah and Tom are struck by tragedy.
The Life of Men: Dr Finlay is faced with an inner conflict between medical ethics and legal obligations when a patient confesses to murder on his deathbed.
Rumpole and the Learned Friends: Rumpole's conduct is questioned when he accuses a police officer of framing his latest client.
Change of Command: Colonel Henry Blake has left the 4077th and Frank Burns relishes the time when he will become the new commanding officer.
It Happened One Night: Hawkeye and BJ are stuck with the overnight shift. Hot Lips tells Frank that she keeps all his love letters so he ransacks her tent to get them back.
The Late Cpt Pierce: The Army mistakenly lists Hawkeye as dead, so he takes advantage of the situation until he discovers that the dead don't get mail call and worse, they don't get paid.
To cheer themselves up Jacko and Elmo go off to a night spot for a drink and to sample the local talent.
Jacko finds himself out of a home. Eric and Jean are moving to Reigate. Veronica leaves for Sicily with Marcello, and Elmo is seen leaving the casino in his pink car with girlfriends.
Painter and decorator Jacko gets into a spot of bother after forgetfully arranging two dates on the same night. At least they're in the same pub!
